metadata.title: SWAG DataLoader Implementation — Wire calibration_data to SWAGPlugin
metadata.priority: P0 — SWAG is a factorial level, must work before GCP launch

plan.problem:
SWAGPlugin requires calibration_data = {"train_loader": DataLoader, "model": nn.Module} but BOTH execution paths skip SWAG because calibration_data is never populated:
1. Parent flow path (GCP): train_flow.py:_run_swag_post_training() passes calibration_data=None → validate_inputs() fails → SWAG skipped 2. Standalone path (local): run_factorial_post_training() has no calibration_data parameter → explicitly skips SWAG with warning
Result: SWAG never runs anywhere. The "3rd pass validation" only tested SWAG in isolation (unit tests), never end-to-end through the flow.

architecture.path: training_subflow() produces: model, train_loader, checkpoint_dirs → _run_swag_post_training() receives training_result → Build calibration_data from training_result fields → Pass to SWAGPlugin via PluginInput

run_factorial_post_training() receives: checkpoint_paths, methods → NEW: also receives calibration_data (optional) → When method=="swag" and calibration_data provided: call SWAGPlugin → Script builds DataLoader from splits, model from model_family

task.title: Test: _run_swag_post_training passes calibration_data to SWAGPlugin
task.description:
Write test that _run_swag_post_training() constructs a PluginInput with calibration_data containing "train_loader" and "model" keys, extracted from the TrainingFlowResult.
Test should mock SWAGPlugin and verify validate_inputs() receives non-None calibration_data with the correct keys.
task.assertion: plugin_input.calibration_data is not None "train_loader" in plugin_input.calibration_data "model" in plugin_input.calibration_data

task.title: Implement: training_subflow returns model + DataLoaders in result
task.description:
Modify TrainingFlowResult to carry the trained model and train DataLoader so post_training_subflow can reuse them. Add optional fields: - trained_model: nn.Module | None - train_loader: Any | None (DataLoader type)
In training_subflow(), after train_one_fold_task() completes, store the model and loader in the result.

task.title: Implement: _run_swag_post_training builds calibration_data from result
Verify: parent flow SWAG path runs end-to-end (mock)
task.description: Run existing + new tests for post_training_subflow. Verify SWAG is no longer skipped. Mock the actual SWAG training (no GPU needed for unit test).

task.title: Test: run_factorial_post_training accepts calibration_data parameter
task.description:
Write test that run_factorial_post_training() accepts an optional calibration_data parameter. When provided with methods=["swag"], it should NOT skip SWAG (currently it does).
Mock SWAGPlugin to avoid GPU. Verify the plugin is called with the provided calibration_data.

task.title: Implement: add calibration_data to run_factorial_post_training
task.description:
1. Add calibration_data: dict[str, Any] | None = None to signature 2. Replace the SWAG skip block (lines 523-531) with actual SWAGPlugin call:
elif method == "swag": if calibration_data is None: raise ValueError( "SWAG requires calibration_data with 'train_loader' and 'model'. " "Build DataLoaders from splits before calling." ) plugin = SWAGPlugin() plugin_input = PluginInput( checkpoint_paths=checkpoint_paths, config=plugin_cfg, calibration_data=calibration_data, ) errors = plugin.validate_inputs(plugin_input) if errors: raise ValueError(f"SWAG validation failed: {errors}") output = plugin.execute(plugin_input) # ... handle output
Note: raise ValueError, NOT logger.warning + skip. Rule 25: loud failures.

task.title: Implement: local script builds DataLoaders for SWAG
task.description:
In phase_1_post_training(), build train DataLoader from fold-0 splits:
from minivess.data.loader import build_train_loader from minivess.data.splits import load_splits from minivess.adapters.model_builder import build_adapter
splits = load_splits(SPLITS_FILE) fold0_train = splits[0].train train_loader = build_train_loader(fold0_train, data_config, batch_size=2, cache_rate=0.0)
For each loss: model_config = ModelConfig(family=ModelFamily.MONAI_DYNUNET, ...) adapter = build_adapter(model_config) calibration_data = {"train_loader": train_loader, "model": adapter.model}
results = run_factorial_post_training( ..., calibration_data=calibration_data, )

task.title: Verify: local 3-flow script runs SWAG successfully
task.description:
Run: MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 uv run python scripts/run_local_debug_3flow.py
Verify: - Phase 1 produces 8 post-training variants (4 losses × {none, swag}) - No SWAG warnings - MLflow runs have post_training_method="swag" tags - SWAG model files exist in outputs/post_training/

task.title: Verify: make test-staging passes with all changes
Verify: dry run of factorial script with new loss count
task.description: ./scripts/run_factorial.sh --dry-run configs/factorial/debug.yaml → 32 trainable + 2 zero-shot = 34 total → POST_TRAINING_METHODS=none,swag in each job

risk-analysis.risk: TrainingFlowResult carrying model + DataLoader may cause serialization issues if Prefect tries to serialize the result across process boundaries. Mitigation: Use Prefect's `allow_failure()` or mark fields as non-serializable.
SWAG training (swa_epochs=2 for debug) adds GPU time per job. DynUNet SWAG on RTX 2070 Super: ~30s per epoch × 2 = ~1 min per condition. Total: 4 losses × 1 min = ~4 min extra for Phase 1.
Memory: keeping model + train_loader in TrainingFlowResult while post_training_subflow runs. DynUNet is small (~5.6M params, ~22MB). SAM3 models are larger (~648M params). May need explicit cleanup.
